subject: Re: Warp/M TensorFlow update
body:
Hi Xingyi,

I've tried Quadro RTX 6000 along with a Gtx 1080Ti on a workstation running 
Warp, everything runs smoothly, no particular issue you need pay attention 
to. Maybe this would be helpful for you? Also, have you find out a way to 
work around the latest 30xx series issue? We just bought a big nice thing 
with 4x 3090, but the particle picking process is extremely slow..

Thank you very much in advance!

Yours sincerely,
Linhua Tai

On Monday, January 31, 2022 at 5:21:50 AM UTC-5 xingyichen...@gmail.com 
wrote:

> Hey Alister,
>
> Thank you so much for your prompt reply! 
>
> Great to hear all 20-series are working! However, since many of them were 
> discontinued, we will consider going for Quadro RTX 6000 which is 
> compatible with TF1 and also Turing grade. 
>
> Would greatly appreciate it if anyone could share their experience with 
> running Warp/M on Quadro RTX 6000 or have other recommendations!
>
> Cheers,
>
> Cyan 
>
> PhD student
>
> The Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>
> On Mon, 31 Jan 2022 at 10:25, Alister Burt <alist...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Cyan,
>>
>> I think lots of people are facing the same issues, not all features which 
>> use TF work with the newer GPU models.
>>
>> All 20- series cards work in our hands
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>>
>>
>> On 31 Jan 2022, at 08:42, Cyan Ching <xingyichen...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>> Dear Warp/M users,
>>
>> Anyone happened to have benchmarked Warp/M performance on different GPUs? 
>>
>> Any recommendations on GPUs that have robust Warp/M performance based on 
>> your experience?
>>
>> Best,
>>
>> Cyan Ching
>>
>> PhD student
>>
>> The Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>>
>> On Fri, 28 Jan 2022 at 13:29, Cyan Ching <xingyichen...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Dear Warp/M users,
>>>
>>> Good morning/day/evening!
>>>
>>> Does anyone know if there will be a TensorFlow update of Warp and M any 
>>> time soon? My lab is looking at purchasing new machines with RTX 3090 GPUs, 
>>> but if the TensorFlow issue persists, we'd consider other options. Besides 
>>> P6000, do you have more recommendations (preferably newer models) that have 
>>> robust performance for running both Warp and M?
>>>
>>> Thank you for your help in advance!
>>>
>>> Best,
>>>
>>> Cyan Ching
>>>
>>> PhD student
>>>
>>> The Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>>>
>>>
>>>
